content: Have you ever wondered why we have different seasons? It's because the Earth rotates on its side as it moves around the Sun. Imagine you're holding a big beach ball and tilting it as you move it around a lamp. The part of the ball that's tilted towards the lamp gets more light and heat, and that's why it's summer in that area. When the part of the Earth where you live is tilted towards the Sun, it's summer and it's warm. When it's tilted away, it's winter and it's cold. In the spring and fall, the Earth is tilted just right, so it's not too hot or too cold. Next time you play outside, remember that the Earth is always moving and tilting, and that's what makes the seasons change! Isn't it cool how our planet moves to create different seasons for us to enjoy?
